articles.title: Baltimore County residents debate data centers at public hearing
articles.description: Baltimore County residents packed a public input hearing Thursday night to share their views on the impacts of data centers as part of the Planning Board’s report process. Outside the Jefferson Building in Towson, protesters rallied against the facilities, chanting, “Communities not corporations!” Joshua Long, a Baltimore County resident, joined the rally, expressing concerns. “Data centers seem to be very disruptive, noise pollution, air pollution, rising energy costs. There just doesn't seem to be that kind of benefit,” Long said. “It is concerning. I'm not anti-tech or anti-AI, but I'm pro-people, pro-Earth.” Earlier this year, the Baltimore County Council passed a data center study bill requiring the Planning Board to submit a report. The report must include things like an assessment of the average electric and water usage of a data center, along with its potential impacts. Thursday’s public input hearing was part of that process.
